guide.title: Peptide Guide
guide.subtitle: A practical reference for researchers working with peptides in controlled laboratory environments. All materials are intended for research use workflows only.
guide.quickTitle: Quick Answer: What are Research Peptides?
guide.quickBody: Research peptides are synthesized short chains of amino acids utilized exclusively for in vitro laboratory studies. To maintain structural stability, they require lyophilization, cold-chain storage, and careful reconstitution using bacteriostatic water prior to experimental application.

topics.title: Peptide Fundamentals
topics.summary: A practical introduction to amino-acid chains, sequence notation, and why peptides are central to controlled laboratory workflows.

topics.title: Synthesis & Purification

topics.summary: Recommended handling protocols for lyophilized compounds and reconstituted solutions, including contamination controls.

topics.title: Quality Verification
topics.summary: How to read HPLC/LC-MS documentation, assess batch suitability, and maintain repeatability across experiments.

cards.scopeBody: In vitro and analytical laboratory workflows only.
cards.complianceBody: Researchers are responsible for local regulatory compliance and approved handling.
